About ProcurePro
Mission > To save 1 BILLION construction admin hours.
What we do > Our market leading technology helps major builders contract with their supply chain by consolidating fragmented and manual procurement processes into a single platform (currently solved by Word, Excel & email).
Recent News: Funding → by Australia’s premier venture capital fund Airtree, recently raised $6.15m in 2023: https://shorturl.at/vUpBX
2024 & 2025 LinkedIn Top Startups, https://tinyurl.com/y5eeucww
Put on Unicorn watch list of QLDs startups, https://shorturl.at/53yWH
Deloitte’s Tech Fast 50 in 2025 - https://tinyurl.com/4p7t72bn

Values we live by
Better people build better companies
Whole arse it, with ruthless pragmatism
Help the customer win
(We’ll tell you more about these as discussions progress)

Our Product-Led strategy
We’re Founded by Developers and Designers (as well as domain experts) - technical excellence is in our DNA. This means:
We spend time up front properly designing new work, testing with customers etc, whilst solving fun technical problems.
We continually invest in testing/quality - we’ve written 10,000+ automated tests in the last 2 years, we're huge advocates for static analysis & type safety, and our infrastructure is automated with Terraform.
Our entire team (including our CEO) is bought into this mindset, as we're reaping the rewards of shipping quality code, avoiding re-work & moving fast as a team.
We're actively rebuilding our processes to be AI-native, not just AI-assisted, and we want engineers who wants to help shape this transition

Our Tech Stack
Backend is Symfony 6 + PHP 8
Frontend is 100% Typescript + Aurelia
Some AWS Lambda functions written in Typescript
Dozens of different testing, code quality and static analysis tools
Infrastructure is managed with Terraform on top of AWS
You don't need experience in all of these technologies to succeed - we look for curious folks with growth mindsets and an interest to work with this stack.

Key aspects of this role
Ship quality features end-to-end > own work from shaping through to deployment and beyond, with tests written as part of the process, not bolted on after
Be an active voice in design phase > contribute during the upfront design phase alongside developers, designers, the product lead, and users; your technical perspective should influence what gets built and how
Use AI as a force multiplier > leverage AI tooling throughout your entire workflow, from investigation to implementation to review; the expectation is fluency, not occasional use
Maintain and raise the quality bar > contribute to our testing culture, engage with static analysis and type safety tooling, and help us keep the codebase in a state we're proud of
Help grow the team around you > work closely with junior developers, share context generously, and contribute to a culture where the whole team gets better
Think critically, not just technically > push back on unclear requirements, ask why before writing code, and bring your judgment to product and architectural decisions, not just implementation

You’re ideal for the role if you have…
2+ years as a software engineer > ideally in a SaaS product environment where you've owned features end-to-end
AI Native Fluency > you use AI tools throughout your entire workflow, not just when you're stuck; you can talk concretely about how you use them & where you apply judgment
Modern frameworks > Experience/familiarity with modern Javascript/TypeScript frameworks (Vue / React / Angular / Aurelia) and/or modern PHP (Symfony / Laravel / CodeIgniter) - we expect some up-skilling/cross-skilling
Quality-obsession > you're excited by test-driven development, static analysis, and building things properly; quality isn't a chore to you, it's how you work
Enthusiastic problem solver > excited by solving technical challenges and building a product with a heavy focus on quality (test-driven development, infrastructure as code etc)
Critical thinking skills > you challenge vague specs, understand the problem before reaching for a solution, and bring genuine reasoning to decisions
Growth-oriented and mission-driven > you're hungry to improve, open to feedback, and want to grow with a scaling business; you thrive in a fast paced, ambiguous environment

Our culture
Remote First - work from anywhere in Australia - we were remote-first before Covid, have established processes around collaboration/culture to ensure inclusiveness & engagement.
Socials - We have regular online social events, quarterly in person socials & hold annual off-sites in person.
Team First - we’re close knit, highly collaborative, actively help each other succeed (knowledge sharing, training) to develop our teams’ capabilities
High Expectations - we’re self motivated, embrace velocity as our greatest advantage, take pride in what we do and push to reach our potential.
Radical Transparency - we believe in flat structures & sharing knowledge, everyone is privy to what happens day-to-day and nothing is hidden (i.e. company strategy, finances & all).
Extra Leave Days - A day off for your Birthday and an extra day off before Christmas

Compensation
$100k-120k + Super (salary based on experience)
Computer equipment allowance to ensure you have everything you need to be successful working remotely

How to apply
Hit Apply and fill out the form with your information, CV and answer to the question "Why do you want to work for ProcurePro?”

What happens next
Shortlisted applicants will have an introductory call [20 mins]
You’ll receive a short assessment to complete [~ 2 hours]
Assessment presentation and discussion call [~1.5 hour]
If successful, we will check references & make you an offer!

Extras:
All our interviews are recorded and shared internally with the CEO and hiring manager. We do this so that our team can make better decisions.
Applications are reviewed on a rolling basis, so speed is key. The sooner you apply, the better!
If you love the sound of working for us and the role described but the title/salary doesn’t align to your experience, please don’t let that stop you applying. We celebrate diversity and we want people of all genders, races, ages, backgrounds, beliefs, and life experiences to join our team.
We complete police checks or the equivalent once you join as part of our SOC2 compliance requirements.
We cannot sponsor visas. You must be based in the country of the advertised role and have the right to work independently. Otherwise, we cannot proceed with your application. Yes, we are remote, but we can only employ in the country advertised.

About Quantaco

Hospitality moves fast the tools should keep up too. At Quantaco, we’re building technology that empowers hospitality businesses to work smarter, not harder. No more disconnected systems that slow you down and create extra work.

Australia’s only locally owned and operated Hospitality Management System, designed to simplify processes, save time, and deliver actionable insights for venues of any size.

Our suite of software and service solutions is optimised to work best together but designed to function independently, delivering the actionable insights and financial reporting pubs, clubs, and restaurants need to thrive.

We empower businesses through expert accounting services, real-time reporting, and connected hospitality operations tools. These tools simplify work, save time, and help venues make decisions that drive growth whether it’s one venue or one hundred.

About The Role

The primary purpose of the Software Developer is to manage the design, installation, testing and maintenance of software systems for our Cooking the Books (CTB) design suite.

This role will be responsible for excellent technical support and the development of software improvements for existing CTB programs or providing entirely new programs for release to the market.

What You’ll Do

Maintain and improve the performance of CTB’s existing software and maintain its’ websites, in-house hardware and network systems

Test, maintain and recommend software improvements to ensure strong functionality and optimisation

Review current systems and identify ideas to improve system performance and impact availability, including cost proposals

Facilitate root cause analysis of system issues and faults;

Resolve complex technical design issues;

Test new products or software improvement releases in a controlled environment before going live;

Proactively seek to continuously understand the design characteristics offered by key competitors; developing design concepts that surpass competitors;

Work closely with analysts, designers and staff to ensure programs operate effectively;

Assist with the preparation of training manuals for users;

Analyse user requirements and convert requirements to design documents;

Provide comprehensive support to internal customers, including achieving resolution to outstanding problems or issues;

Develop and implement new software;

Assist the Marketing department with preparation of digital design collateral in line with CTB’s brand guidelines;

Automate repetitive technical tasks

About You

You are organised and a fast learner

You have a mature, design-led approach to software development

Integrity, honesty, trust, and respect

Self-motivated, but you love working in a team

Innovative and always looking for ways to improve

You’re an effective communicator, patient, and able to explain your point of view

Have a degree in Computer Science or equivalent

Have 2+ years’ experience in C# and.Net Framework;

Possess strong technical knowledge of web technologies (Javascript, CSS, HTML, etc.);

Are proficient in C#, ASP.NET MVC and Web API

Have knowledge of ASP.NET Core, ADO.net and ORMs (EntityFramework, Dapper.net, NHibernate)

Have knowledge of Databases: SQL Server, PostgreSQL (Store Procedures, and NoSql: Redis and MongoGB)

Are proficient in JavaScript frameworks: AngularJs, VueJs, and ExtJs

Possess knowledge of Microsoft Azure cloud platform (App Service, Service Bus, Service Fabric)

Have experience with Source Code Control: SVN and GIT

Have a demonstrated understanding of projects from the perspective of both client and busines

Possess proficient verbal and written communication skills;

Have effective time management skills and the ability to meet deadlines

Have the ability to solve problems by being collaborative, creative and analytical

Why Join Us?

At Quantaco, we’re passionate about building technology that solves real-world problems in hospitality. Our team is collaborative, curious, and committed to making an impact and we want you to grow with us.

Reward: Competitive salary + up to 20% discretionary performance bonus
Wellbeing: Hybrid working, birthday leave, and wellness discounts through our benefits portal
Culture: Fortnightly collaboration days, social calendar, team events, peer recognition, and a strong commitment to inclusion
Growth: Study assistance, a structured development framework, and our Women at Quantaco initiative to support diverse career progression

Ready to build the future of hospitality tech?

Apply now or reach out for a confidential chat. We’d love to meet you.

Quantaco Pty Ltd, Level 4 / 6 O'Connell Street Sydney NSW 2000

Be YOU

Recognised for delivering projects of global and national significance that help keep Australia safe, it is our shared passion that shapes our culture, and our people that make it a great place to work.

We have a great opportunity for multiple Software Engineers to join our Strategic Surveillance Program based at Adelaide – South Australia or Melbourne Central – Victoria, reporting to the Senior Software Engineer.

We embrace flexible work arrangements at BAE Systems Australia, such as working a 9-day fortnight and or hybrid working.

You will bring with you demonstrated previous skills and experience in the development of specialised software systems. Within our HF Export programs, you’ll work on areas such as UX development, SW design and architecture, and signal processing. We have a growing team with the opportunity to constantly learn and carve out your niche in an area that interests you. You’ll have interactions with many other teams and stakeholders, as well as engineers in other disciplines, and have opportunities to travel internationally. We’re a strong team that takes pride in what we do and how we work together, with a lot to offer a software engineer looking to expand their skillset.

Key responsibilities include

Conduct software engineering in accordance with the Software Development Plan
Writing high quality, maintainable software including generation of design and test documentation
Active participation in all Scrum ceremonies each sprint, including planning, stand-ups, reviews, and retrospectives, contributing to continuous improvement and team collaboration.

About YOU

Strong understanding of the Software Development lifecycle
Knowledge/experience in one or more of the following technologies:
Strong working knowledge of C/C++ Programming language (preferably on Linux)
Full Stack development with strong front-end and back-end development skills (HTML, CSS, JavaScript, React, or similar modern frameworks).
Experience with RESTful APIs, authentication, and third-party integrations.
Experience with a variety of communications libraries, e.g. Apache Kafka and DDS, in environments with very high data throughput
Expertise in Containers, Kubernetes
Strong working knowledge of programming on a Linux framework
Development experience with the Qt framework is desirable
Experience as part of an agile/scrum team
Familiarity with Configuration and Change Management principles and tools, such as Git
Strong analytical and troubleshooting skills
Defence security clearance or eligibility to obtain a NV1 clearance
Eligibility to be cleared for International Traffic In Arms (ITAR) regulations
We believe in hiring for potential, we know that some candidates hesitate if they don’t tick all the boxes. If this role interests you, but you don’t meet all the criteria, we still encourage you to apply.

About US

You will be joining a workplace that truly cares about your wellbeing.

Our people are diverse and we value the variety of perspectives this brings, we know that a culture that is inclusive and fosters a sense of belonging is a place we can all make the most of our unique talents and be our best.

As Australia’s largest defence company in a rapidly growing sector, you will be connected into a global team and encouraged to explore where your career might take you whether that is a traditional career path or squiggly one, there are infinite possibilities for you at BAE Systems Australia.

BAE Systems recognises the sacrifices of those who have served our Nation and their families. We support our veteran community through our internal veteran network (VetNet Australia) and strong charitable partnerships, internal offerings including reserve service leave, a Veteran Mentoring Program and other exclusive offerings available through our strategic partners.

Clearances 

As a Defence security clearance is required for this role, applicants must be Australian citizens and either possess or be eligible to obtain and maintain appropriate clearances. Further information regarding security clearances.

International Traffic In Arms Regulations (ITAR) are applicable and as such, your nationality may be a factor in determining your suitability for this role. Please visit this webpage for more information.

About the job
About Zero to Claw
Zero to Claw is a white-glove OpenClaw setup and automation service. We deploy security-hardened, production-ready OpenClaw AI agents for businesses. Our clients range from solo operators who want a tireless digital assistant to small teams who need agents handling email triage, meeting prep, CRM workflows, and cross-app automation.
Our founders bring deep experience: Jake has led $100M+ in business transformation, and Daniel is a former Atlassian Engineer with 6+ years shipping Jira, Bitbucket, and Confluence to millions of users.

The Role
We need someone to help us deliver custom OpenClaw workflows for our clients and maintain their running instances. You'll be the person who takes a client's messy "I want my AI to do X when Y happens" request, figures out how to wire it up in OpenClaw, builds it, tests it, and makes sure it keeps running. You'll also handle troubleshooting when things break — a misconfigured MCP server, a flaky WhatsApp integration, a gateway auth issue, an agent that's burning through API credits. You'll be deploying, configuring, integrating, and debugging real systems for real clients on real deadlines.

What you'll do
Connect OpenClaw to client tools — Gmail, Notion, Slack, Jira, Salesforce, databases, and hundreds more
Build custom workflows and skills when off-the-shelf integrations don't cut it
Troubleshoot live client instances — broken integrations, runaway API costs, flaky messaging channels, agent misbehaviour
Talk to clients, understand what they need, and translate that into working automation

What will help you succeed
Comfort in a terminal — SSH, config files, logs, basic server admin
Some experience with any programming language — enough to read code, write scripts, and hit APIs
Basic understanding of networking — ports, firewalls, DNS
Familiarity with Docker
Clear communication — you'll explain technical things to non-technical people regularly
A bias toward figuring things out over waiting to be taught

Bonus points
You've played with OpenClaw, MCP, or any AI agent framework
You've deployed something to a VPS or cloud instance before
You know your way around monitoring tools (Langfuse, Grafana, etc.)
You have opinions about LLMs, tokens, and model routing
You've built a bot, a home server, a Raspberry Pi project, or anything that proves you like tinkering

You don't need to tick every box. If you're curious, resourceful, and excited about AI agents — we'd rather hear from you than not.

Why join us
You'll get production experience with AI agents that most people only read about. You'll work directly with two founders — one from Atlassian engineering, one from enterprise strategy — who'll give you real responsibility from day one, not busywork. And you'll be joining at the ground floor of a company in one of the fastest-moving spaces in tech.

To apply
Send an email to join@zerotoclaw.ai with a short note about why this interests you, your resume/LinkedIn, and ideally one example of something technical you've built or deployed (a side project, a home server, a bot, anything that shows you can ship).

About the job
About Fat Zebra
At Fat Zebra we simplify payments so that merchants and platforms can scale with confidence. Our flexible, secure, and scalable payments platform supports over 100,000 merchants across Australia and New Zealand. We help our customers navigate the complexity of the payments ecosystem so that they can focus on what matters most - growing their business.

About The Opportunity
As a fast-growing payments scale-up, we’re continuously evolving our technology and investing in the future. The Data & AI team is building a new, modern data platform to deliver observability and insights to our internal and external customers. We have a new role for a Data Engineer to add to our foundation team; someone who can turbo charge our capacity for ingesting events, building data products, and providing data insights to our consumers. As we progress with our strategy, there will also be opportunities to develop analytic and agentic capabilities to layer on top of the foundational data products.

As a key member of our Data & AI team, you will contribute and collaborate on the following:
Build and maintain robust data pipelines from source systems into lakes and warehouses.
Negotiate and develop processes and data contracts for data pipelines.
Deliver curated, API-ready data products.
Develop opinions about architecture and ways of working.
Reconcile data sources with transformed data products.
Implement best practices for coding standards, CI/CD, and testing.
This is a hands-on role with real impact and is ideal for someone who has a passion for data and wants to shape a data platform from the ground up while contributing to a broader Data & AI strategy.

In addition, you will have the opportunity to contribute to analytics, insights, and emerging agentic capabilities that optimise services for our merchants and customers.

About You
We are looking for someone with a product mindset and big aspirations. Someone who is curious, driven, and excited to build something that is future focused and benefits merchants, customers and Fat Zebra. Someone who:
is excited by the challenge of creating new architecture and pipelines,
has a passion for building in a testable and extensible way,
understands cloud data infrastructure as it relates to scalable event ingestion and processing,
is comfortable with event‑driven architectures and streaming or near‑real‑time workloads, and
has a bachelor’s degree or equivalent years of work experience in a field like Computer Science, Software Engineering, Data Science, Data Engineering, Networking & Cloud Computing.

Required Skills
Experience with Python, SQL, Typescript, and event-based systems at scale
Working knowledge of AWS and cloud-based data infrastructure
Experience building and maintaining robust data pipelines
Understanding of modern data architectures (OLTP/OLAP, pipelines, streaming)
Familiarity with CI/CD, testing practices, and infrastructure as code.

Nice To Have
Experience with event-based systems at scale
Experience in the payments industry
Experience with Ruby on Rails
An interest in building agentic systems to deliver factual insights over real data.

Why Join Fat Zebra?
Be part of a fast-growing Australian Payments company scale-up
Work on a greenfield data platform transformation
Hybrid working arrangements
Supportive, people-first culture
Opportunity to shape the future of Data & AI at Fat Zebra.

Curious? Read the PD, ask questions we’d love to hear from you.

For a confidential conversation please contact Talent Manager, Rose Maugeri on 0409236061 at Fat Zebra.

About the job
Job Description

DXC Technology (NYSE: DXC) is a leading enterprise technology and innovation partner delivering software, services, and solutions to global enterprises and public sector organisations — helping them harness AI to drive outcomes at a time of exponential change with speed. With deep expertise in Managed Infrastructure Services, Application Modernisation, and Industry-Specific Software Solutions, DXC modernises, secures, and operates some of the world’s most complex technology estates. Learn more on dxc.com

What You Will Be Doing

DXC Managed Cyber Services (MCS) is the specialist, Digital Security division within DXC Technology. Our team provides a broad portfolio of security services offering end-to-end operational management of market-leading technologies and security services for local and multinational clients.

As part of our continued focus on the Australia and New Zealand market, we are enhancing the Security Engineering Team who work within the Secured Infrastructure capacity to deliver security services at the highest standard for our customers.

Only candidates able to gain or currently holding a current National Police Clearance and Australian Federal Government Security Clearance at Baseline will be considered for this role. Australian Citizenship is mandatory.

Who You Will Be Working With

DXC Security is one of the few companies in Australia & New Zealand that can provide end-to-end security solutions—from expert advisory services to fully managed security operations, enabling the delivery of complete remediation plans and security improvement programs.

Responsibilities

To facilitate the onboarding, management, and troubleshooting of the cybersecurity services
Assist in high-priority incidents including both infrastructure and security-related
Ensure security services—including policies, configuration, and compliance—meet or exceed industry best practices
Fulfill daily operational tasks to ensure all managed services meet Service Level Agreements (SLAs), actively identifying opportunities to automate repetitive administrative tasks.
Implement and execute tasks to fulfill the project requirements
Keep up-to-date with a variety of security technologies, trends, and emerging AI-driven security tools
Liaise with vendors and their technical support/engineering team to provide advanced-level support

The Skills You Will Bring

At least 2 years’ experience working in IT/OT security operations for medium and/or large enterprises.
Experience and good knowledge in implementation and/or managing and troubleshooting some of the following vendors’ technologies. NGFW – Palo Alto/CheckPoint/Fortinet/Cisco
Experience working in cloud environment including Amazon Web Services/Microsoft Azure
Experience in high-priority incident response
Holder of relevant industry/vendor certifications such as CISSP, Microsoft AZ-900, AWS Certified Cloud Practitioner, Palo Alto PCNSE, CheckPoint CCSE, Fortinet NSE 7, or Cisco CCNP.
Understanding of the OSI Layer model from TCP/IP to application level and having the ability to troubleshoot & run analysis.
Strong team player with a can-do attitude who works well collaboratively

Highly Desirable (But Not Essential)

Automation & Scripting: Proficiency with high-level programming or scripting languages (such as Python or PowerShell)
Infrastructure as Code (IaC): Knowledge of deploying and managing network security controls using IaC tools (e.g., Terraform, Ansible)
API Integration: Experience utilizing RESTful APIs to automate firewall rule provisioning, policy management, and reporting
Artificial Intelligence: Familiarity with leveraging AI technologies (e.g., AI-assisted scripting tools, automated threat intelligence, or AI-driven SIEM analytics) to enhance operational efficiency

Our Culture And Benefits

DXC is committed to building better futures for our customers, colleagues, environment, and communities. We take care of each other and foster a culture of inclusion, belonging and corporate citizenship. We put this to action developing and implementing societal initiatives within our Social Impact Practice. #WeAreDXC

As an employer of choice, our “people first” philosophy means we offer competitive remuneration, benefits, training, and career opportunities that reflect our commitment to improving the lives of our employees, and the communities in which we live and work.

 How to apply & our commitment to you in return

In return, we agree to ensure a hiring process that is enjoyable, thorough, and fair. We strive to provide an environment that lets you thrive and show off the very best version of yourself, while learning about us at the same time.

We are an Equal Opportunity Employer

DXC is proud to be an equal opportunity employer and we welcome submissions from people from all walks of life. We celebrate our diversity and recognise it is the unique contributions of our people that give us our edge. We stand by the ‘bring your whole-self to work’ philosophy. It is our inclusive culture that powers our results, and our company grows only if our people grow.

Accommodation of special needs for qualified candidates may be considered within the framework of the DXC Accommodation Policy. In addition, DXC Technology is committed to working with and providing reasonable accommodation to support qualified individuals with physical and mental disabilities.

At DXC Technology, we believe strong connections and community are key to our success. Our work model prioritizes in-person collaboration while offering flexibility to support wellbeing, productivity, individual work styles, and life circumstances. We’re committed to fostering an inclusive environment where everyone can thrive.

Recruitment fraud is a scheme in which fictitious job opportunities are offered to job seekers typically through online services, such as false websites, or through unsolicited emails claiming to be from the company. About the job
Who We Are:

Spawnpoint Media is at the forefront of digital entertainment, spearheading innovation in content creation across YouTube channels and media brands within the realms of gaming, Minecraft, and Live Action content. With a passionate community of over 23 million followers, we've amassed more than 6 billion views and over 57 thousand years of content watched globally. We strive to maintain the soul, love, joy, and personality of youth in our content and are committed to our mission of leaving a lasting impact on families across the globe.

Who You Are

Our team is seeking a skilled and creative Java Game Developer, who can work with our incredible team of Minecraft Set Designers and Java Developers to bring YouTube scripts and storyboards to life in an interactive and exciting way through custom Minecraft Mods.

This is a casual position, working remotely.

Your Responsibilities And Skills

Minecraft Mod Programming: Develop and compile Minecraft: Java Edition mods based on provided YouTube scripts, encompassing various elements such as blocks, items, mobs, entities, UI, commands, and custom gameplay features.
Custom Functionality Implementation: Design blocks, items, mobs, and entities with unique functionalities and AI behaviour, enhancing gameplay dynamics within Minecraft.
UI and Command Customization: Create custom Minecraft UI elements and commands to enrich the player experience and introduce new gameplay mechanics.
Crafting Methods and Recipes: Implement custom crafting methods and recipes beyond the default Crafting Table, introducing novel crafting mechanics and item interactions.
Engaging Gameplay Design: Craft interesting and unique gameplay moments and level designs within the scripts, ensuring audience engagement and interest throughout the Minecraft experience.
Video Storyboard Scripts: Identifying mod requirements and tasks from video storyboard scripts
Collaborative Set Design Support: Collaborate with the Set Design team to develop mods that complement and enhance the physical set designs, facilitating efficient and practical script implementation.
Integration with Modellers: Work closely with Modellers to integrate code and gameplay mechanics into tangible Minecraft models, bringing virtual elements to life within the game environment.
Audience-Centric Mod Development: Develop mods that resonate with the core audience of the videos, primarily young children, by ensuring visual appeal and engaging gameplay functionalities.

Requirements:

Minimal requirement - v1.20+ modding experience
Proficient in Java programming language
Developing Minecraft mods for NeoForge in Java, typically on Minecraft 1.20.1 utilising existing/pre-provided assets
Must be familiar with Eclipse and/or Intellij Java IDEs.
Full understanding of Minecraft and all game mechanics
Translate design ideas into functional game code
Troubleshooting and rectifying issues with developed mods
Understands how to navigate and read through technical documentation online in order to advance one's understanding of the subject matter and/or resolve bugs.
Ability to interpret instructions and create modded content from pre-existing visual assets in a relatively quick turnaround
Ability to investigate and resolve basic modding issues both in your own code and others
Ability to read and interpret Minecraft crash reports and log files to identify issues
Familiarity with GeckoLib and its usage as a dependency in modding
Ability to understand feedback and act on it.

Preferable Additional Skills

Understands what a JSON file is and how to create or edit one (loot tables, tags, etc)
Experience working in Java Edition/Datapacks/1.17+ Command Syntax/NBT data
Understands basic Minecraft related development terms, such as entity, datapack, addon, behaviours, components, function, animation controllers, etc
Understands basic programming logic/design (scoreboard loops, execute if’s, operators, etc).
Familiar with Blockbench (or Cubik Studio/some other modelling program)
Familiar with Aseprite, Paint.net, Photoshop, or some other form of editing software.
Ability to make small modifications or changes to existing models/assets in the event it is needed

Spawnpoint Perks:

Attractive Remuneration
Spawn Points KPI bonus Program
Training & Development
Career Progression

If you’re excited to develop some of the most-loved content on the internet and bring smiles to millions of young people across the globe; whilst working alongside a highly skilled and fun-loving team; passionate about all things YouTube and gaming, then what are you waiting for? We really love working here, and we think you will too! Apply now!
